title: When worry about climate change leads to climate action: How values, worry and personal responsibility relate to various climate actions
authors: Thijs Bouman; Mark Verschoor; Casper J. Albers; Gisela Böhm; Stephen D. Fisher; Wouter Poortinga; Lorraine Whitmarsh; Linda Steg

tax_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: increasing taxes on fossil fuels, such as oil, gas and coal (tax fossil fuels)
tax_mentions.context: Support for “increasing taxes on fossil fuels, such as oil, gas and coal” (ESS8 item) modeled as outcome of biospheric values, climate worry and personal responsibility.
tax_mentions.page_or_section: Methods 2.2.2 Outcome variables: Climate policy support
tax_mentions.notes: 5-point support scale; higher score = stronger support; included in regression/mediation with country-level standardization.

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: using public money to subsidise renewable energy such as wind and solar power (subsidize renewables)
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: Provides government subsidies for renewables; no tax on fossil fuels/carbon and thus not a carbon/fuel taxation instrument.
excluded_policy_mentions.page_or_section: Methods 2.2.2 Outcome variables: Climate policy support

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: a law banning the sale of the least energy efficient household appliances (ban least energy efficient appliances)
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: Ban on sales of least energy-efficient appliances is a regulation/standard, not a carbon or fuel tax.
excluded_policy_mentions.page_or_section: Methods 2.2.2 Outcome variables: Climate policy support

inclusion_reason: Uses ESS Round 8 item on support for “increasing taxes on fossil fuels, such as oil, gas and coal” and models determinants (biospheric values, climate worry, personal responsibility) across 23 countries.
